title: mangifera andamanica
intro: Mangifera andamanica, also known as the Andaman mango, is a species of mango tree native to the Andaman and Nicobar Islands in India. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The leaves of Mangifera andamanica are rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds, which have been found to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, contributing to its health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. The leaves can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, decoctions, and infusions, which can be consumed fresh or dried to harness their medicinal properties.
